hypothesis: A powered, multi-seed dose sweep of semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight (0/2/6), holding the rest of the E619/E620 standard recipe fixed, will show a real dose-response effect on OOD quality metrics (E626/PR#625's deferred next step, applied to a lever already present on this branch instead of duplicating its new required_slot_margin_decode_weight lever).
falsifier: The three dose levels produce byte-identical predictions across all seeds/records, and code-level instrumentation shows why.
method: Trained 3 fresh 800-step scratch twotower checkpoints (seeds 0, 1, 2; same corpus/architecture as E619/E620) and replayed the full E619/E620 standard eval recipe (schema_role_slot_decode_weight=8, semantic_role_decode_weight=8, slot_coverage_close_decode_weight=2, schema_value_decode_weight=4, schema_opaque_close_decode_weight=4, semantic_plan_decode_weight=4, semantic_plan_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_binding_decode_weight=1, semantic_plan_root_decode_weight=8, semantic_plan_root_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_repeated_array_close_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_repeated_slot_margin_decode_weight=2, semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight=2 (fixed, NOT swept), honest_slot_contract/slot_contract_constrained_decode/slot_contract_in_context=true, grammar_ltr_max_tokens=160) 3 times per seed with only semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight varied at 0, 2, 6 (9 real eval runs, OOD n=4 each, 36 predictions total). Combined the 3-seed pooled outcomes with the committed SLM-183/H19 statistical protocol (slm_training.evals.power_protocol: wilson_interval, bootstrap_paired_ci, intraclass_correlation) for a real powered read, then added temporary (uncommitted, not shipped) instrumentation around TwoTowerModel._semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_bias to trace the exact decode-time mechanism, and ran one additional confirmatory arm with BOTH semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight and semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight set to 0 together (a true full ablation) to close the loop.

training.note: seed0's loss (4.068013) reproduces E620's seed-0 800-step loss (4.068010) to 4 decimal places; the checkpoint sha256 differs from E620's own artifact because this is a fresh training invocation (metadata/timing are not bit-identical across sessions), consistent with prior sessions' 'faithful replay' standard in this lineage (loss match, not byte-identical weights file).

eval_recipe.matched_to: E619/E620's full evaluation_policy (the same fixed weights both iterations record), with only semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight varied and 3 fresh seeded checkpoints instead of 1

byte_identical_predictions_check.result: all 36 predictions byte-identical across margin in {0,2,6} for every seed/record

power_protocol_analysis.method_ref: slm_training.evals.power_protocol (wilson_interval, bootstrap_paired_ci, intraclass_correlation), the same statistical primitives wired by SLM-183/H19 (docs/design/iter-slm183-power-protocol-20260720.md), applied here to real multi-seed eval records for the first time (prior H19 usage was synthetic-fixture or single-seed analyze-existing only)

icc_meaningful_v1_by_seed.note: negative ICC on n=3 clusters of 4 is a small-sample estimation artifact (within-seed variance exceeds between-seed variance here), not evidence of a real negative correlation; not over-interpreted.

paired_bootstrap_delta_fidelity_margin6_.note: degenerate [0,0] CI because every paired record is byte-identical between margin=0 and margin=6, not because the sample size was too small to detect an effect

power_protocol_analysis.seed_variance_note: Real, substantial seed variance exists independent of the margin sweep: pooled meaningful_v1 0.4167 masks 0.50 (seed0), 0.50 (seed1), 0.25 (seed2); mean fidelity 0.55 (seed0), 0.6333 (seed1), 0.625 (seed2). Seed is the dominant source of variance in this recipe, not the swept margin weight.

instrumentation_trace.method: Temporary (uncommitted) monkeypatch wrapper around TwoTowerModel._semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_bias counting calls/returns and logging owner_family/target-token on every non-None return, run against the seed0 checkpoint with margin=6.
instrumentation_trace.root_cause: The function's own target selection (twotower.py _semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_bias, ~line 5107-5121) branches on `semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight` (typed_margin), NOT on the swept `semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight` (margin): when typed_margin > 0 (true throughout the E617-E620 standard recipe, fixed at 2.0), `targets` collapses to the single schema-derived minimal-completion id (`state._minimal_schema_id(schemas[0])`) regardless of `margin`'s value. `margin` only enters via `max(margin, typed_margin)` as a shared bias-MAGNITUDE ceiling (line ~5126-5131), and in this checkpoint/example the bias needed to flip the local argmax toward the object-open token was already reached at magnitude 2 (i.e. by typed_margin alone) at the one place this bias fires across all 3 seeds x 3 dose levels x 4 records. Raising margin to 6 cannot change which candidate wins once its target is already argmax at margin=2 -- more headroom above the runner-up does not change the winner.

ablation_confirmation.config: semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight=0 AND semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight=0 (both zeroed together, unlike the swept arms which held item_margin fixed at 2)
ablation_confirmation.finding: When BOTH weights are truly zero, ImageGallery reverts to closing its typed array empty -- E610/E612/E616's already-rejected failure mode -- and fidelity/validity/reward drop measurably (0.55->0.4667, 0.73->0.58, 0.814->0.6267) while structure/AST/recall are unchanged. This confirms semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight (not the swept nonempty-margin weight) is the one doing the real work of keeping this array nonempty in the standard recipe.

decision: Reject sweeping semantic_plan_typed_array_nonempty_margin_decode_weight in isolation as a way to find a dose-response effect in the standard recipe: as long as its sibling semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight stays active (true in every E617-E620 recipe), the swept weight's own target-selection branch never executes, and its only remaining contribution (a shared magnitude ceiling via max()) is already saturated at this checkpoint scale. This is a genuine implementation coupling, not a training or gating bug (contrast with E617's silent-no-op bug, which was a missing flag). No checkpoint promoted or synced. Not a ship claim.
next_step: To actually test a dose-response margin effect, either (a) sweep semantic_plan_typed_array_item_margin_decode_weight itself (the lever that actually drives target selection here), or (b) zero both nonempty/item margins together as the control arm before sweeping nonempty_margin upward, so the two levers are not confounded via max(). Separately, E620/E626's open 'required slots still missing after full coverage' and 'Input.name role swap' findings remain unaddressed by this iteration.
test_plan: 3 real 800-step CPU scratch trains (seeds 0/1/2), each under the 3-minute cap
9 real matched OOD n=4 eval runs (3 seeds x 3 margin doses), each under the 3-minute cap
1 additional real ablation eval run (both weights zeroed)
python -m scripts.verify_version_stamps --check -> ok, 0 components touched (no source changed)
